# UC-13.6.4 · Grafana Plugin Installation and Version Mismatch Detection

## Description

Highly available Grafana clusters with skewed plugin versions produce phantom panel errors that reproduce on one node only. Comparing reported plugin versions across hosts catches incomplete rollouts before users open tickets.

## Value

Eliminates flaky dashboard behavior during rolling upgrades and supports change audit evidence.

## Implementation

Augment with nightly API scrape `/api/plugins` ingested as JSON with `sourcetype=grafana:plugins`. Alert when `ver_dc>1` for `plugin_id` in the same `stack` field. Exclude dev hosts via lookup.

## SPL

```spl
index=observability (sourcetype="grafana:log" OR sourcetype="grafana:audit")
| search plugin OR "plugin*version"
| rex field=_raw "(?i)plugin[=_](?<plugin_id>[a-zA-Z0-9._-]+)"
| eval ver=coalesce(plugin_version, version)
| stats values(ver) as versions, dc(ver) as ver_dc, latest(_time) as t by plugin_id, host
| where ver_dc > 1
| sort -ver_dc
```

## Visualization

Table (plugin × host × version), Treemap of drift count, timeline of plugin audit actions.

## References

- [Grafana — Plugins](https://grafana.com/docs/grafana/latest/plugins/)
- [Splunk Lantern — use case library](https://lantern.splunk.com/)
